MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR LEAVE TO OBTAIN DISCOVERY BY MENTAL EXAMINATION (TRANSACTION ID # 66337093) FILED BY DEFENDANT SADAT, SAYED TARIQ AN INDIVIDUAL

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT SAYED SADAT'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO OBTAIN DISCOVERY BY PHYSICAL OR MENTAL EXAMINATION: AFTER HAVING READ THE MOVING PAPERS AND DOCUMENTS IN SUPPORT, CONSIDERING THE APPLICABLE LAW, FACTS, AND ARGUMENT SUBMITTED BY MOVING PARTY, AND THE FACT THAT THE REQUEST WAS UNOPPOSED, THE COURT HEREBY GRANTS DEFENDANT SADAT'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO OBTAIN DISCOVERY BY PHYSICAL OR MENTAL EXAMINATION. BECAUSE THE RESPONSE WAS WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THE DATE OF THE DEMAND (SEE CCP 2032.230), THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING VIA EMAIL TO JYJUDGEPROTEM@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: JASON YURASEK, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT SAYED SADAT'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 8/9/21 IS VACATED AND THE NEW TRIAL DATE IS 1/24/22 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. PARTIES WAIVED NOTICE. MR. SHEM TO SEND NOTICE OF TRIAL TO ALL PARTIES. MR. SHEM TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT IS APPROVED AS TO FORM PURSUANT TO CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS DEPOSITION AND FOR AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 66220364) FILED BY DEFENDANT SADAT, SAYED TARIQ AN INDIVIDUAL

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT SAYED SADAT'S MOTION OF SAYED SADATS MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF, MARIE HURABIELL, TO APPEAR FOR ORAL DEPOSITION IS UNOPPOSED AND GRANTED. WITHIN TEN DAYS OF SERVICE OF A COPY OF THIS ORDER WITH NOTICE OF ENTRY THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER TO SET A FIRM DATE FOR DEPOSITION QUESTIONING WHICH SHALL TAKE PLACE NO LATER THAN THIRTY DAYS THEREAFTER OR SUCH OTHER LATER DATE AS THE PARTIES SHALL AGREE. THE COURT DECLINES TO AWARD A MONETARY SANCTION PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 2023.030(A) BASED ON MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS IS DEFINED IN C.C.P. 2030.010. THE SINGLE EMAIL SENT TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL REQUESTING A DEPOSITION DATE IS INSUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT A SANCTION AWARD ON THAT BASIS. SEE SPECIFICALLY C.C.P. 2023.030 (I) AND THE OMISSION OF EMAIL FROM THAT STATUTE SUBSECTION. PREVAILING/MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 VIA EMAIL TO SBS@SBSLAWSF.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVE STEIN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice And Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Sayed Sadats Supplemental Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And Award Of Monetary Sanctions And-Or Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAYED SADAT's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Sayed Sadats Supplemental Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And Award Of Monetary Sanctions And-Or Terminating Sanctions is Transferred to be heard on the discovery calendar (Dept. 301) on the same date at 9:00 a.m.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Defendant Sayed Sadat'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Sayed Sadats Supplemental Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And Award Of Monetary Sanctions And-Or Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Defendant Sayed Sadat's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Sayed Sadat's Supplemental Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And Award Of Monetary Sanctions And-Or Terminating Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion is granted. Plaintiff shall serve code compliant responses to Supplemental Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents within 10 days.

Sanctions in the amount of $750 are awarded against Plaintiff.

The prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and email it to the Judge Pro Tem at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Atty. CHRISTOPHER COLE, Esq. of Law Office of Christopher Cole (Tel: 415-279-9897) for plaintiff Marie Hurabiell;

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Notice And Notice Of Motion For Leave To Obtain Discovery By Physical Or Mental Examination
Off Calendar Mar-09-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAYED SADAT's Motion For Leave To Obtain Discovery By Physical Or Mental Examination:

After having read the moving papers and documents in support, considering the applicable law, facts, and argument submitted by moving party, and the fact that the request was unopposed, the Court hereby grants Defendant Sadat's Motion for Leave to Obtain Discovery by Physical or Mental Examination. Because the response was within 20 days of the date of the demand (see CCP 2032.230), the request for sanctions is denied. The moving party to submit a proposed order for the Court's approval and signature.

Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling via email to jyjudgeprotem@gmail.com.

Notice And Notice Of Motion Of Sayed Sadats Motion To Compel Deposition
Off Calendar Feb-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Mark Shem for Moving Party/Defendant, Sayed Sadat.
No other appearances.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAYED SADAT's Motion Of Sayed Sadats Motion To Compel Deposition:

Defendant's motion to compel plaintiff, Marie Hurabiell, to appear for oral deposition is unopposed and granted. Within ten days of service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry the parties shall meet and confer to set a firm date for deposition questioning which shall take place no later than thirty days thereafter or such other later date as the parties shall agree. The Court declines to award a monetary sanction pursuant to C.C.P. 2023.030(a) based on misuse of the discovery process. Misuse of the discovery process is defined in C.C.P. 2030.010. The single email sent to plaintiff's counsel requesting a deposition date is insufficient to support a sanction award on that basis. See specifically C.C.P. 2023.030 (i) and the omission of email from that statute subsection.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steve Stein, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
